Fetch the supported M7 data subset

Source: R/fetch-public.R
gx_fetch.Rd

Executes the six frozen handler families—direct CSV, WQP Result, EDR position, current USGS continuous, current USGS daily, and OGC API Features—in deterministic global order. Failures are isolated to one distribution and remain visible in the returned status table.

Usage

gx_fetch(plan, parallel = 1L, dry_run = FALSE)

Arguments

plan

A validated gx_fetch_plan from gx_fetch_plan().

parallel

Must be exactly 1L; parallel execution is not part of the frozen M7 contract.

dry_run

If TRUE, return planned statuses without provider work.

Value

A gx_fetched object. $status contains one row per distribution; $results contains one row per successful handler-native payload with data and retained raw_body list-columns; $provenance retains the validated bounded execution contract.

Details

Execution is deliberately sequential and single-page. Each response is capped at 8 MiB, one call admits at most 32 requests and 64 MiB, tabular payloads are bounded to 100,000 rows, 1,000 columns, and 1,000,000 fields, and OGC API Features requests use a 10,000-feature page ceiling. Redirects, retries, and cache reuse remain disabled by the underlying execution contract. Latest and legacy USGS variants, page following, runtime handler registration, serialization, and replay are deferred rather than silently attempted.

A dry run performs the same planning and admission without package probing, DNS, provider transport, clocks, throttling, cache access, or writes.

On this page

geoconnexr Experimental software; verify results before operational use.

Developed by Kyle Onda. Site built with pkgdown 2.2.1.
